tracks.meaning: to exercise power or influence over something or someone; to direct or manage
a mechanism or interface used to regulate or operate a device or system
the ability to manage one's emotions, impulses, or behavior in a disciplined way
a standard or condition imposed by an authority to regulate conduct or activity
a software feature or program that monitors and regulates system operations or user access
the act of tracking, monitoring, or directing military forces, weapons, or operations
authority or responsibility for managing or overseeing a company, department, or operation
the regulation or oversight of financial transactions, budgets, or expenditures
the ability to direct or steer a conversation toward a desired outcome or topic
the management or regulation of a disease, condition, or bodily function through treatment
to have someone under one's power or influence; to dominate
a sample or substance used as a reference for comparison in an experiment
the ability to direct, steer, or guide the path or motion of an object or vehicle
the technical management of stage equipment, lighting, sound, and other production elements
